stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
Key technologies involved include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
This indicates active community interest and usage of Meta's latest instruction-tuned model.

stories.title: TSMC struggles to keep up with AI demand: ‘We can only support so much’
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: TSMC, the dominant semiconductor manufacturer, is facing significant challenges in meeting soaring demand from AI customers.
The company stated that despite ongoing factory expansion, its current capacity has limits in supporting the high volume of orders.
This struggle highlights the critical bottleneck in the global supply chain fueling the current AI boom.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging applications with local AI coding agents.
The key feature is running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It simplifies the setup process by eliminating the need for environment variables or config files.
The tool supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: Elon Musk tries again to escape FTC audits of X data handling
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Elon Musk is facing scrutiny from the FTC regarding data handling practices at X.
Public commenters express distrust in Musk's ability to safeguard user privacy on the platform.
The story highlights ongoing regulatory challenges for X concerning data compliance.

stories.title: LatentSkill: From In-Context Textual Skills to In-Weight Latent Skills for LLM Agents
stories.author: Jianghao Lin
bullet_points: LatentSkill addresses the context overhead and plaintext exposure issues of using textual skills in LLM agents.
It converts reusable textual task procedures into plug-and-play LoRA adapters via a pretrained hypernetwork.
This allows skill knowledge to be stored in model weights rather than context space, enabling modular and efficient loading.

stories.title: Anthropic's open-source framework for AI-powered vulnerability discovery
stories.author: binyu
bullet_points: Anthropic has released an open-source framework.
The framework is designed for AI-powered vulnerability discovery.
This signals a move toward democratizing AI security research.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance Claude Code's real-time information access.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Amazon’s new plan for games: James Bond and AI Snoop Dogg
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Amazon's gaming strategy has historically lacked clear definition.
The company has been actively involved through acquisitions (Twitch), cloud services (Luna), and MMO investments.
Amazon leverages its existing content library (Prime Video/MGM) to bolster its gaming presence.

stories.title: The skeptic’s guide to humanoid robots going viral on the Internet
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: The article addresses the potential for viral robot demonstrations to create skewed public perceptions of actual robotic capabilities.
It serves as a critical analysis ('skeptic's guide') rather than reporting a new breakthrough.
The topic is relevant to the growing public interest and hype surrounding humanoid robotics.

stories.title: These LLMs are the best at resisting Russian propaganda
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: An Estonian government benchmark was used to test the resilience of various Large Language Models (LLMs).
The study specifically evaluated how well these models resist 'strategic narratives' originating from Russia.
The findings indicate that certain LLMs demonstrate superior capabilities in combating Russian propaganda.

stories.title: Dashlane explains how attackers managed to download encrypted password vaults
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dashlane revealed the method used by attackers to compromise encrypted password vaults.
The attack strategy involved targeting a large volume of users to increase the probability of a successful breach.
The story addresses a critical cybersecurity vulnerability related to password management tools.

stories.title: How some data center operators are tackling their water use problems
stories.author: Molly Taft, wired.com
bullet_points: Data center operators face increasing scrutiny regarding their water consumption.
The environmental impact of hyperscalers on local water availability is a growing concern.
The article discusses various strategies being implemented by these operators to mitigate water usage issues.

stories.title: Where does Absolute Position come from in decoder-only Transformers?
stories.author: Fabrizio Silvestri
bullet_points: The paper investigates how decoder-only Transformers derive absolute positional information despite using RoPE (Rotary Position Embeddings) which primarily encodes relative offsets.
Two architectural components are identified as sources of this 'absolute position leakage': the causal mask and the residual stream.
The causal mask's softmax denominator is dependent on the absolute query position, and the residual stream contributes the second source of absolute positional encoding.

stories.title: IR3DE: A Linear Router for Large Language Models
stories.author: Oğuzhan Ersoy
bullet_points: The paper introduces IR3DE, a linear router designed to select the most appropriate Large Language Model (LLM) for a given prompt.
It addresses limitations in existing inference routers, which either focus on cost optimization across generalist models or require extensive training for domain-specific routing.
The research aims to improve the efficiency and specialization of using large collections of LLMs.

stories.title: OrderGrad: Optimizing Beyond the Mean with Order-Statistic Policy Gradient Estimation
stories.author: Yutaka Matsuo
bullet_points: Introduces OrderGrad, a new family of gradient estimators for policy-gradient methods.
Addresses limitations of standard methods by optimizing distributional properties of returns (e.g., tail risk, robustness) rather than just expected return.
Allows optimization of finite-sample L-statistics, enabling objectives like VaR, CVaR, and trimmed means in reinforcement learning.

stories.title: SkillComposer: Learning to Evolve Agent Skills for Specification and Generalization
stories.author: Junbo Zhao
bullet_points: SkillComposer addresses the limitation of current agent skill construction, which treats skill creation as a one-shot extraction problem.
The research tackles the trade-off between task-specific skills (poor generalization) and abstracted skills (insufficient guidance).
The core contribution is introducing explicit mechanisms for skill specification to improve skill evolution and transferability.

stories.title: Multi-task Learning is Not Enough: Representational Entanglement in Dual-output Second Language Speech Recognition
stories.author: Young-Min Kim
bullet_points: The study investigates dual-output second-language (L2) speech recognition, requiring both pronunciation and intended meaning.
It challenges the standard assumption of Multi-task Learning (MTL) by showing it fails across Korean and English.
MTL improves semantic meaning but degrades surface transcription, with degradation scaling based on Levenshtein distance in English.

stories.title: VoidZero Is Joining Cloudflare
stories.author: coloneltcb
bullet_points: VoidZero is announcing a partnership or integration with Cloudflare.
This suggests a potential synergy between VoidZero's technology and Cloudflare's infrastructure.
The news originates from Hacker News, indicating interest within the developer/tech community.

stories.title: Kaggle is making AI benchmark creation effortless
stories.author: nicholas_kangnick_ac18
bullet_points: Kaggle is introducing new features to simplify the creation of AI benchmarks.
The focus is on supporting the evolution of AI models into complex reasoning agents.
These agents are capable of advanced tasks like writing code and utilizing external tools.
